title: Clarify BR applicability
summary: Documented the Bonhomme-Robin applicability boundary for the bivariate L=K=2 SVAR and defined the manuscript object as a BR-style profiled inversion.

user_inputs.text: Continue with M07: prove what the Bonhomme-Robin analogy does and does not justify by showing why full BR quasi-JADE does not directly apply for the bivariate L=K=2 SVAR and documenting the manuscript object as a BR-style profiled inversion.

agent_actions.description: Created manuscript/derivations/br-applicability.md showing why direct all-kurtotic Bonhomme-Robin quasi-JADE fails when independent bivariate errors give J=1<K=2.
Documented that the skewness route also does not cover the two-factor L=2 case and separated what the BR analogy justifies from what it does not.
Updated the formal-object registry, dashboard, task board, workplan, paper map, review log, session log, Codex log, and matching GitHub milestone.

checks.description: python scripts/check_manuscript.py: passed with only the expected open-milestone warning before M0009 closure
python scripts/check_manuscript.py: passed after M0009 closure
